heading: Edit a MongoDB Vector Search Index
body:
You can change the [index definition](avs-types-vector-search)
of an existing MongoDB Vector Search index from the Atlas UI, Atlas Administration API,
Atlas CLI, [mongosh](mongodb-shell), or a supported [MongoDB Driver](drivers).
You can't rename an index or change the index type. If you need to
change an index name or type, you must create a new index and delete the
old one.

You can change the [index definition](avs-types-vector-search)
of an existing MongoDB Vector Search index using [mongosh](mongodb-shell), compass, or a supported
[MongoDB Driver](drivers). You can't rename an index or change the
index type. If you need to change an index name or type, you must create
a new index and delete the old one.

You can't modify an `autoEmbed` type field after you create the index.
You can modify the index to add or remove `filter` type fields.

After you edit an index, MongoDB Vector Search rebuilds it. While the index rebuilds,
you can continue to run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using the old index definition.
When the index finishes rebuilding, the old index is automatically
replaced. This process is similar to MongoDB Search indexes. To learn more,
see [upgrades your search indexes](index-create-and-update).
